highlights.text: I wrote back on Monday at 4pm (before the sell) that: “ Bear case tomorrow: Short-term, begins below 6453” and “If 6453 fails, we start down.” Tuesday morning, ES lost 6453 and began a sell that took ES down to 6362 by Wednesday morning.
I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
As readers of this newsletter know - while I provide short entries to readers - I don’t short myself as I solely trade the subsequent squeezes on the longside via Failed Breakdowns.

Therefore, selloffs like we had to start this week (where ES sold 145 points from last Friday’s high into Wednesday’s 6361 low) are of interest to me because they - without exception - produce an equally violent rally to the upside, driven by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, and its what we saw today.
As readers know very well though, every “elevator down” sell in ES (which is what we saw Wednesday morning) has a sibling: The short squeeze.
When ES sells, it cuts through every level for a few minutes to a few hours, it induces panic, then it puts in a vertical squeeze on a Failed Breakdown.
ES flushed under 6375 (this was roughly yesterdays daily low) down to 6363, trapped, Failed Breakdown, then after 10am today, squeezed to that 6461 target, and beyond.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the rare Breakdown Short setup we had that caused Tuesday’s 100+ point sell (this was given to readers in advance) then I’ll discuss the subsequent Failed Breakdowns we saw.

post_title: The Dip Was Decisively Bought in SPX. Are New All Time Highs Coming? Or Bull Trap? August 25 Plan
